---
title: "Day 9: Depart Naples to Brindisi"
subtitle: "Fly Naples → Rome → Brindisi — Arrive Puglia 6:25pm"
date: "2026-06-13"
day-of-week: "Saturday"
location: "Naples → Rome Fiumicino → Brindisi"
categories: [itinerary, travel, naples, puglia, brindisi]
---

## Overview

**Travel day:** Two short Alitalia (ITA Airways) flights take us from Naples to Rome Fiumicino, then on to Brindisi — gateway to Puglia. We arrive at the heel of the boot by 6:25pm with an evening ahead.

**Today's Flights:**

| # | Flight | Route | Departs | Arrives | Notes |
|---|--------|-------|---------|---------|-------|
| 1 | AZ 1270 | NAP → FCO | 3:05pm | 4:05pm | Terminal not assigned; check in advance |
| 2 | AZ 1623 | FCO → BDS | 5:15pm | 6:25pm | Terminal 1 at FCO |

**Reservation Code:** IXYZMT | **Airline Code:** BY4EV5 (AZ)

**Checked baggage:** 2 × 23kg per person on both flights
**Carry-on:** 1 × 8kg per person

---

## Morning: Last Hours in Naples (8 AM–12 PM)

### Final Naples Morning

The flight isn't until 3:05pm, so you have a real morning in Naples. Don't squander it.

**Options:**
- Final espresso and *sfogliatella* at a neighborhood bar — standing at the counter, the right way
- **Napoli Sotterranea** (Underground Naples) — if you haven't done it, this morning is your window
  - Guided tours depart throughout the morning from Via dei Tribunali
  - 90-minute tour; ancient Roman theater + WWII air raid shelters 40 meters underground
  - Book ahead: [napolisotterranea.org](http://www.napolisotterranea.org)
- A final walk on the Lungomare for Vesuvius views across the bay
- Or simply pack, check out, and have a relaxed café morning

### Hotel Checkout

- Aim to check out by noon; ask for luggage storage if you want more morning time
- Settle the bill and check for errors
- Confirm taxi to airport

---

## Getting to Naples Airport (12:30–2:30 PM)

### Napoli Capodichino Airport (NAP)

**Located:** ~6 km from the historic center

| Option | Time | Cost | Notes |
|--------|------|------|-------|
| **Taxi** | 20–30 min | €20–25 | Official white taxis; best with luggage |
| **Alibus shuttle** | 30–45 min | €5 | From Piazza Garibaldi; stop outside Centrale station |

**Depart hotel by 1:30pm** — the airport is small but check-in closes 45 minutes before departure.

### At the Airport

- [ ] Check in and drop bags — check-in closes 2:20pm for the 3:05pm flight
- [ ] Clear security (small airport — usually quick)
- [ ] Confirm departure gate on boards
- [ ] Grab lunch or coffee airside — there are decent options

::: {.callout-note}
## Terminal Note
Naples Airport doesn't have separate named terminals — one building. Check the departure board for your gate. AZ 1270 to Rome Fiumicino.
:::

---

## Flight 1: Naples → Rome Fiumicino (AZ 1270)

**Airline:** ITA Airways (Italy's national carrier, formerly Alitalia)
**Duration:** 1 hour
**Cabin:** Economy

- Departs NAP 3:05pm
- Arrives FCO (Rome Fiumicino) **4:05pm**, Terminal 1
- Short domestic hop — essentially just a climb and descent

---

## Rome Fiumicino Connection (4:05–5:15pm) — 70 Minutes

**Layover at FCO Terminal 1**

This is a tight but manageable connection within the same terminal.

- [ ] Follow signs for **Domestic/Schengen connections** within Terminal 1
- [ ] Check departure boards for AZ 1623 to Brindisi gate
- [ ] No need to re-clear security or passport control (both flights are domestic/Schengen)
- [ ] Grab water and a snack — there are options in T1 airside
- [ ] Be at gate by 4:50pm

::: {.callout-warning}
## Connection Timing
70 minutes is workable at Fiumicino within the same terminal. Don't stop to browse shops — find the gate first, then relax if you have time.
:::

---

## Flight 2: Rome → Brindisi (AZ 1623)

**Airline:** ITA Airways
**Duration:** 1 hour 10 minutes
**Cabin:** Economy

- Departs FCO Terminal 1, 5:15pm
- **Arrives BDS (Brindisi Papola Casale Airport), 6:25pm**
- Collect checked bags at Brindisi baggage claim

---

## Arrive Brindisi (6:25pm)

### Brindisi Airport — Papola Casale (BDS)

Small, easy airport — nothing like Naples or Rome.

**After landing:**
1. Baggage claim (small airport — bags come quickly)
2. Exit to arrivals — taxi stand immediately outside

### Getting to Your Hotel

**Taxi:**
- ~€12–15 to Brindisi centro
- ~10–15 minutes
- Official taxis are at the stand outside arrivals

---

## Evening: First Night in Puglia (7:30–10 PM)

Welcome to the heel of the boot. The cuisine changes immediately — you're no longer in Naples.

**Puglia staples that start tonight:**

| Dish | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Orecchiette** | "Little ears" pasta, often with turnip greens (*cime di rapa*) or ragù |
| **Burrata** | Fresh mozzarella filled with cream — Puglia's most famous gift |
| **Fave e cicorie** | Fava bean purée with bitter greens — ancient, extraordinary |
| **Primitivo** | Local red wine — rich and full-bodied, from Manduria |
| **Negroamaro** | Another Puglian red — darker, more tannic |

**Where to eat:** Walk toward the inner harbor and look for family-run trattorie on Corso Garibaldi or the streets into centro storico. Avoid anything immediately adjacent to the tourist waterfront.

### Optional: Walk the Inner Harbor

**Brindisi's waterfront** is compact and walkable — even after a travel day:

- **Colonne Romane** — Two Roman columns (one intact) marking the end of the Appian Way; where ancient Rome ended. Julius Caesar, Cicero, Augustus walked here.
- **Seno di Ponente** — The sheltered inner harbor; fishing boats and Mediterranean ferries
- **Castello Svevo** — Norman-era castle overlooking the harbor

::: {.callout-note}
## Easy Does It Tonight

You've done two flights and spent a week each in Florence and Naples. Tonight is dinner, a short walk, and sleep. Puglia starts tomorrow.
:::

---

## Prepare for Tomorrow

**First full Puglia day:**

- [ ] Review [Puglia Overview](../regions/puglia.qmd) for context on the region
- [ ] Confirm tour pickup time/meeting point (if applicable)
- [ ] Confirm accommodation for next several nights
- [ ] Get cash at an ATM if needed (easy to find in centro)
- [ ] Charge all devices overnight

---

**Previous:** [Day 8: Naples Museums & City](day-08-2026-06-12-naples-herculaneum.qmd) | **Next:** [Day 10: First Day in Puglia](day-10-2026-06-14-naples-to-brindisi.qmd)
